name: Parque Natural de la Serra d'Espadà — Northern Tour from Caudiel
name_original: Parque Natural de la Serra d'Espadà — Vuelta norte desde Caudiel
region_intro: Road cycling around Caudiel offers diverse landscapes, ranging from gentle slopes to challenging mountain passes. The region is characterized by its varied topography, including the Ojos Negros Greenway, a repurposed railway path, and proximity to the Sierra Calderona and Sierra de Espadán Natural Parks. These natural features provide a mix of terrains suitable for different cycling skill levels, with demanding climbs and scenic views. The area's natural beauty extends to several other attractions, making it a compelling destination for active outdoor experiences. Best road cycling routes around Caudiel The most popular road cycling route is Parque Natural de la Serra d'Espadà — Northern Tour from Caudiel, a 31.6 miles (50.9 km) difficult trail that takes 3 hours 1 minute to complete. This route explores the northern sections of the Sierra d'Espadà Natural Park, featuring significant elevation gains. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Montanejos – Arenos Reservoir loop from Jérica - Viver, a difficult 50.0 miles (80.5 km) path. This route offers varied terrain around the Arenos Reservoir, with sustained climbs and descents. Local road cyclists also love the Historic center of Jérica – Jérica loop from Jérica - Viver, a 27.0 miles (43.5 km) trail leading through historic areas and surrounding landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 20 minutes. Road cycling around Caudiel is defined by mountain passes, natural parks, and greenways. The network offers options for both moderate and difficult ability levels. The routes in Caudiel are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 2000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Caudiel's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Caudiel?
A: There are over 80 road cycling routes around Caudiel, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. You'll find a mix of moderate and difficult trails to explore.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Caudiel?
A: Caudiel's road cycling terrain is quite diverse, featuring everything from gentle slopes to challenging mountain passes. You'll encounter sections of the &lt;strong&gt;Ojos Negros Greenway&lt;/strong&gt;, a repurposed railway path, and routes that traverse the varied topography of the &lt;strong&gt;Sierra Calderona&lt;/strong&gt; and &lt;strong&gt;Sierra de Espadán Natural Parks&lt;/strong&gt;. Expect significant elevation gains on many routes.

Q: Are there routes suitable for beginners or families in Caudiel?
A: While Caudiel is known for its moderate to difficult road cycling routes, there are options that cater to those looking for less strenuous rides. You can find 27 moderate routes, which might be suitable for cyclists with some experience or families looking for a less challenging day out. The &lt;strong&gt;Ojos Negros Greenway&lt;/strong&gt;, though part of longer routes, offers flatter sections.

Q: Where can I find scenic viewpoints or natural attractions along the routes?
A: Many routes offer stunning natural features. The region is rich in natural beauty, including the &lt;strong&gt;Sierra Calderona&lt;/strong&gt; and &lt;strong&gt;Sierra de Espadán Natural Parks&lt;/strong&gt;. For specific highlights, consider routes that pass near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1310997" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bride's Leap Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; in Navajas or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2099448" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tío Juan Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;strong&gt;Fuente de los Baños - Montanejos Lake&lt;/strong&gt; is also a popular spot.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by historical sites or castles?
A: Yes, some routes will take you through areas with historical significance. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38216256" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic center of Jérica – Jérica loop from Jérica - Viver&lt;/a&gt; leads through historic areas. You can also find routes near landmarks like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2304606" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic center of Jérica&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/646697" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almonecir Castle&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5361128" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of the Virgin of Pradas and Castle of Pradas&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Caudiel?
A: The region generally offers good cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn typically provide pleasant temperatures for road cycling. Summers can be warm, especially in the midday, while winters are milder but can still have cooler temperatures, particularly at higher elevations.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available from Caudiel?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Caudiel are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31439913" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montanejos – Arenos Reservoir loop from Jérica - Viver&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31481548" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puertomingalvo Pass – Montanejos loop from Caudiel&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are the parking options for road cyclists in Caudiel?
A: Caudiel, being a municipality, offers various parking options, typically in or near the town center. For specific trailheads, it's advisable to check local signage or use navigation apps for designated parking areas close to your chosen starting point. Many routes begin from accessible points within or just outside the town.

Q: How do other cyclists rate the road cycling experience in Caudiel?
A: The road cycling experience in Caudiel is highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.8 out of 5 stars from over 20 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the challenging climbs, and the stunning natural landscapes, particularly within the natural parks.

Q: Are there any challenging routes for experienced road cyclists?
A: Absolutely. Caudiel is an excellent destination for experienced road cyclists seeking a challenge. Many routes feature significant elevation gains and demanding mountain passes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31481548" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puertomingalvo Pass – Montanejos loop from Caudiel&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult route covering over 146 km with substantial climbing.

Q: Are there places to stop for refreshments or food along the cycling routes?
A: Yes, many routes pass through or near small towns and villages where you can find cafes, restaurants, and shops to refuel. The &lt;strong&gt;Fuente de los Baños - Montanejos Lake&lt;/strong&gt; area also has picnic facilities. Planning your route with komoot can help you identify suitable stops along the way.

Q: Is public transport an option for accessing cycling routes in Caudiel?
A: Caudiel is accessible by public transport, primarily by train, as it has a station on the C-5 line of Cercanías Valencia. This can be a convenient option for reaching the area and starting your cycling adventure, though you should check specific train regulations regarding bicycle transport.
tips: Nice and quiet stretch that joins the 2 towns. As advantages, there is practically no traffic and the landscape is very picturesque. As a disadvantage, the surface is somewhat broken and you will have to be careful especially on the descent so as not to hit any potholes.

Section between villages inside the Serra d'Espada Park rolling through some amazing landscapes. The road rarely has car traffic, you will be very calm being able to enjoy the landscape passing under the Alto del Pinar.
